nodes.title: Return original object for empty path
nodes.why: If there is no path to walk, the most natural result is the object itself.
nodes.tradeoff: More lodash-like behavior, but callers must know this edge case.

nodes.title: Accept both array and string paths
nodes.why: Some callers already have path segments, others pass dot/bracket strings.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly more branching, but more flexible API.

nodes.title: Tokenize string paths with one regex
nodes.why: This captures keys from `a.b` and `a[0].b` without manual parsing code.
nodes.tradeoff: Regex is compact and practical, but less self-explanatory at first glance.

nodes.title: Walk safely and stop when path breaks

anchor.snippet: if (cur == null || !(key in Object(cur))) return defaultValue;

nodes.why: Avoids runtime errors and returns fallback as soon as a segment is missing.
nodes.alternative: Use try/catch around direct nested property access.
nodes.tradeoff: Clear control flow, with a tiny check cost per segment.

nodes.title: Treat final undefined as missing
nodes.why: If resolved value is truly `undefined`, callers usually expect the provided fallback.
nodes.alternative: Return `undefined` exactly when property exists but value is undefined.
nodes.tradeoff: More convenient defaults, but it does not distinguish missing vs explicitly undefined.
